author: @technosaurus3805

author: @michaelinamoment
text: I wonder if you could produce a mug with a mold that has the plate sandwiched into the wall of the mug. Having a ceramic outtershell. Looking at how mugs are mass produced they have a single use mold (looks like plaster de paris mix) with enough clay that is for one mug. I think if the plate was pressed to form of the mug before hand, and that it was rigid enough not to move when clay is pressed into the mold you could have the plate inserted into the wall of the mug. I am not sure what that would do in terms of firing, but in terms of product mass production design that could be the angle to look for.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice one mate - cheers

I was thinking last night of making an oven with Pyrex casserole dishes set in a foamed refractory cement, very economic of grid cooking I would imagine but with this l g s it could go straight on the bricks and heat up quicker and make hobs in cement work tops
